steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence lists three films by three different filmmakers: Zoot Suit, El Norte, and Stand and Deliver. A three-item series requires commas between items: item 1, item 2, and item 3. The blank must close Zoot Suit's year and introduce El Norte as a distinct second item.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '(1981) *El Norte* (1983),': No comma after (1981), so Zoot Suit and El Norte run together without any separator. The sentence would read "Zoot Suit (1981) El Norte (1983), and Stand and Deliver" — the first two series items are fused. ✗
  • Choice B — '(1981) *El Norte* (1983)': No comma after (1981) and no comma after (1983). All three items collapse together with no separation at all. ✗
  • Choice C — '(1981)—*El Norte* (1983)—': Paired dashes set off El Norte (1983) as a parenthetical interruption rather than a series item. The result is "Zoot Suit (1981)—El Norte (1983)—and Stand and Deliver" — El Norte is bracketed as an aside, breaking the parallel series of three films. ✗
  • Choice D — '(1981), *El Norte* (1983),': The comma after (1981) separates item 1 from item 2; the comma after (1983) separates item 2 from item 3. The full sentence reads "Zoot Suit (1981), El Norte (1983), and Stand and Deliver (1988)" — correct series punctuation. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
